nts1.d: Welcome to Club Coco: a place where globe-trotting vinyl hunter Coco María shares her joyous discoveries - jazz, fusion, international pop, vintage latin music of all flavours…

A brief overview of notable artists who have created music at the intersection of traditional Spanish flamenco and pop music, from the trends beginnings in the 1970s to the present day. The selection features gipsy artists predominantly, a group whose contribution to Spanish popular music is frequently minimised. 
Selected by Sasha Binia Benarie.

From the host: "In the mix with host HD. This episode of 1080P is for dancing into summer with fresh club and hip hop tracks from around the world."

subtle.d: Representing UK Bass Music Culture across a range of styles, sounds and tempos.

Rolling through varied tempo's and genres, Jimmy 2 Shoes brings dance floor orientated vibes and flows to wriggle your toes to. Expect eclectic selections and the freshest sounds from the UK underground.

Sound Arkitek is a Chicago hip-hop DJ with a love for all things drum & bass. 

With over 20 years experience playing house parties, raves, clubs and bars he has an encyclopedic knowledge of music but has always been a participant in jungle culture. In the early 2000s he was exposed to the genre primarily through Dieselboy and Danny The Wildchild mixes as well as the “Jungle Book” series of mixtapes. He became obsessed after hearing Bad Company’s “The Nine” and “Four Days,” as well as Pendulum’s “Vault.” 

As time went on, he got deeper into the scene, going to raves while buying records and posting on Drum & Bass Arena and DNBForum before turning 21 at which point he began going to Lava Lounge for Bass By The Pound’s “Seminar” series. The first legal drum & bass party he attended was Chase & Status at the aforementioned Lava Lounge, and he will never forget the energy when the duo played DJ Hazard’s “Killers Don’t Die” and it got a rewind. His love for the scene led to him playing drum & bass parties, opening for artists like Original Sin, Gridlok, and LTJ Bukem among others. 

His Subtle Radio show is an hour of the finest tunes in the scene at the moment. 

To keep up, follow @djsoundarkitek on Instagram!

Dubversified showcases Canadian West Coast DJ talent specializing in Bass Music. Hosted by Mirage, founder of Dubversified Culture, a community-led organization focusing on music, art, and events. Mirage, originally from Québec, blends house and hip-hop since 2010, now exploring 140bpm and Deep D&B.

subtle.n: Into The Lion's Den w/ Restrikt

subtle.d: Potent Funk records’ Matt Frost - Well known for his technical abilities and Sleazy bass music style, Frost covers all angles from UK alt Rap to the LA beat scene.

XOSÉ is a DJ and aspiring producer mixing and making mostly UK Garage, Grime and UK Funky. FORWARD PRESSURE largely focuses on the aforementioned UK born genres, though you can expect a variety of UK influenced sounds.

Sempra is a producer & DJ based in London, specialising in the fusion of UK Garage, Jungle and Breakbeat with dreamy vocal melodies and the beautiful instrumentation found in Soul and contemporary RnB. Sempra’s consistent output has been released by labels such as Future Classic and Headroom, gaining industry support from the likes of Nia Archives, Sherelle, Yung Singh and Dr Dubplate.

Cicely is a northern raised London based music photographer and DJ. From learning to mix during lockdown, she has since played at clubs across the country in venues such as Fabric, Corsica and Fold.

Cicely runs a collective and events night under the name NRG CRU which focus heavily on the high energy rave sound. She is highly influenced by the northern sound she grew up with, and you can hear this within her sets. Expect heavy blends of techno and hardcore while she shines the light on her favourite northern artists.

Live & Direct is the show that promises to make you dance and make you laugh, with your fave tunes that make you go 'Rahhh ain't hear this in time' and all the cheesy jokes from me, Hannah :) Lock in for the sickest guests and the most relatable stories about daily slips and hilarious situations I find myself in.

Bristol-born and London-based, Not Bad For A Girl resident DaiSu has been involved in the music scene since 2018 and has featured on stations such as Radio 1 Dance, BBC 6 Music and Melodic Distraction, as well as playing on line ups for Keep Hush, Skiddle and Red Bull. Her penchant for bass, breaks and 2-step has landed her gigs supporting acts such as Interplanetary Criminal, Main Phase, Craigie Knowes and Big Ang. In September 2022, her debut EP ‘Lightwork’ was released with Gather Records and was featured in DJ Mag's staff picks that same month.

Vinyl DJ from Leigh-on-Sea. Loves collecting records with a wide array of taste ranging from House and Techno to Grime, Dubstep and Jungle. Garage holds a special place in his heart and makes up the largest part of my collection and DJ sets.

DJ & Producer hailing from Watford influenced by Hip-Hop, Dubstep, Grime with releases on NSX records & Ghost Phone, spinning electronic genres from R&B edits, Drum and Bass and everything in between.
Highlander brings you 2 hours of the freshest Jungle, Hardcore, Dubstep and Breaks. 3rd Saturday 7-9pm lock in!

subtle.n: The Wheel Up Show w/ Tripta
What's The Weather, Selecta? w/ Megan Wroe

subtle.d:
Hailing from Mumbai, now living in London, Chaitu is energised by the UK sound and scene.

With a love of all things weird, weighty and groovy; his mixes and productions bridge electro, techno, breaks, UKG and Jungle, with sprinklings of everything in between.

Chaitu champions provocative beats that break down the barriers of style, genre and expectation, all while keeping you moving.

dublab.d: “Dying Songs focuses on melancholic electronic music but isn’t limited to that. Sometimes it gets dark, sometimes I try to mix techno awkwardly, sometimes i get nostalgic… I like stuff that’s dreamy, ghostly, pretty and sad. I’ll try out new productions of my own, as well. I’m keeping it pretty open and we’ll see how it evolves.” – Jimmy

Frankie Jax No Mad - Not Evil Radio
Daddy Differently and Dirty Dave - Things of Life
dublab.d: It’s the simple things isn’t it? The little things of life. Good food, sunshine, no traffic, dark coffee, 72˚ breeze, telephone off the hook and music every Friday morning a la Daddy Differently & Dirty Dave.
